title: Cannot assign signals in interface that defines a clocking, even if clocking never used

features.name: interface signal with unused clocking block input reference
features.description: Code should include an interface signal that is referenced as an input in a clocking block, where the clocking block is never used in the design
features.snippet: interface Axi5Lite(input logic aclk, input logic aresetn); logic arvalid; clocking transmitter_clocking @(posedge(aclk)); input arvalid; endclocking endinterface

features.name: non-blocking assignment to signal with clocking block input declaration
features.description: Code should include a non-blocking assignment to an interface signal that is declared as an input in an unused clocking block, causing Verilator to incorrectly warn about blocked and non-blocking assignments to the same variable
features.snippet:
logic arvalid; // interface signal
clocking transmitter_clocking @(posedge(aclk)); input arvalid; endclocking
// Later in module:
interface_inst.arvalid <= 1'b1; // Non-blocking assignment

features.name: interface with output signal in clocking block
features.description: Code should include an interface with a signal declared as output in a clocking block, which Verilator may incorrectly treat as a blocking assignment
features.snippet: interface Ifc(input clk); logic sig; clocking cb @(posedge clk); output sig; endclocking endinterface
